The role focuses on setting up financial records, capital accounts, and reporting infrastructure for new entities, while also supporting existing entities and reinsurance programs.   • Lloyd’s Administration: Support Funds at Lloyd’s (FAL) for the Group’s Syndicate in a Box (SIAB), including FAL registers, asset movements, and reconciliations.

  • Record Keeping: Build and maintain organized financial, regulatory, and corporate records for new entities, aligning with DFSA, CIMA, and Lloyd’s requirements.
  • Claims & Data: Support claims payment processing, commission/statement data entry, and flag discrepancies.

Yarab Technologies (P) Ltd

Capital & Structuring Manager (Qualified) Closing Date: 03, July 2026 Job Published: 01, July 2026 Contact Email: jobs@yarabltd.co.uk

About the Role

We are seeking a qualified capital/finance professional to lead the capital and control workstream for new regulated insurance entities established by the Group — including DFSA, Cayman, and Lloyd’s structures. The primary focus is on new entity establishment: defining the capital structure, building the financial control framework, and establishing the regulatory reporting infrastructure from inception. Once operational, you will support ongoing financial and regulatory requirements, alongside existing entities and reinsurance programs. You will also supervise a junior analyst. This is a build-focused role for someone comfortable standing things up from scratch, not just running steady-state reporting.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead financial establishment of new regulated entities (DFSA, Cayman, Lloyd’s) — define opening balance sheet, capital structure, and financial control framework.
  • Build capital adequacy and solvency models for new entities; maintain and update as entities grow.
  • Establish regulatory reporting frameworks (e.g., DFSA, Cayman, Lloyd’s), including reporting packs, calendars, and initial returns.
  • Build three-statement financial models and entity-level profit/loss structures for management reporting.
  • Establish reinsurance program accounting (quota share, excess of loss, ceding commission) and control frameworks.
  • Produce monthly management accounts and entity-level reporting packs for new entities; support existing programs.
  • Reconcile intercompany balances across group entities.
  • Manage Group’s Funds at Lloyd’s (FAL) position for Syndicate in a Box (SIAB), including capital setting processes, FAL composition, and capital calls.
  • Run capital adequacy stress testing and scenario analysis (shock loss, premium growth, FX sensitivity) to assess SCR/MCR thresholds.
  • Manage collateral arrangements (letters of credit, trust accounts) for reinsurance programs.
  • Own capital waterfall and inter-entity funding mechanics (dividend upstream, loan repayment, capital repatriation).
  • Track reinsurance counterparty credit exposure, maintain registers, and flag risks.
  • Produce board-level capital reporting packs (capital dashboard, solvency headroom, FAL position, stress test outcomes) quarterly.
  • Support existing entities and reinsurance programs as needed.
  • Supervise and direct the Capital & Structuring Analyst.

Preferred Skills

  • Qualified or finalist accountant (ACCA, CIMA, ACA, or equivalent).
  • Experience with capital adequacy, solvency reporting, and regulatory returns in insurance/reinsurance.
  • Hands-on financial modeling in insurance/reinsurance contexts.
  • Understanding of reinsurance accounting and collateral management.
  • Demonstrable experience setting up financial/reporting infrastructure for regulated entities (DFSA/DIFC, Cayman, Lloyd’s).
  • Strong Xero skills and advanced Excel/modeling ability.
  • Comfortable working independently.
summary: Lead capital and control for new regulated insurance entities, building financial frameworks and regulatory reporting. Requires qualified finance professionals with multi-jurisdictional experience.
